info.name: Northstar Bank - Customer Enrollment API
info.description: Northstar Bank uses the Customer Enrollment API to accept, review, and manage digital account enrollment applications across retail banking channels. It is consumed by mobile banking, branch-assisted onboarding, contact-center tools, and approved partner onboarding experiences that need a governed way to submit prospective customer information. The API exists in the Northstar Bank portfolio to standardize enrollment workflows, enforce consistent security controls, and make application status visible across internal and partner journeys.

item.name: E2E Workflow
POST /applications - should create a new application and return 201

request.description: Creates a new customer enrollment application for the E2E workflow.

item.name: GET /applications/:id - should retrieve the created application

request.description: Reads the application created earlier in the E2E flow.

item.name: PATCH /applications/:id/status - should move application to in-review

request.description: Moves the created E2E application into manual review.

item.name: PATCH /applications/:id/status - should approve reviewed application
GET /applications/:id - should verify approved state

request.description: Verifies that the E2E application is approved.

item.name: Smoke Tests
GET /health - should return healthy service status
GET /applications - should list seeded applications

request.description: Happy-path smoke check for listing applications.

item.name: GET /health - should reject invalid API key with 401

request.description: Smoke check proving invalid API keys are rejected.

item.name: CRUD Operations
POST /applications - should create a CRUD test application

request.description: Creates an application for CRUD-specific tests.

item.name: GET /applications - should list applications with filters

request.description: Lists applications with status and product-type filters.

item.name: GET /applications/:id - should return a CRUD application by ID

request.description: Retrieves the CRUD test application by ID.

item.name: PATCH /applications/:id/status - should update CRUD application status

request.description: Updates the CRUD test application status.

item.name: DELETE /applications/:id - should delete CRUD application

request.description: Deletes the CRUD test application from in-memory demo data.

item.name: Negative Tests
GET /applications - should reject invalid API key with 401

request.description: Negative authentication test using an invalid x-api-key value.

item.name: POST /applications - should return 400 for missing required fields

request.description: Negative validation test for a request missing required customer and consent fields.

item.name: GET /applications/:id - should return 404 for unknown ID

request.description: Negative resource lookup test for an unknown application ID.

item.name: PATCH /applications/:id/status - should return 409 for invalid status transition

request.description: Negative business-rule test for invalid lifecycle transitions.

exec: const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();
const suffix = Date.now().toString().slice(-6);
const taxIdLast4 = String(1000 + (Number(suffix) % 9000));
pm.collectionVariables.set('requestTimestamp', timestamp);
pm.collectionVariables.set('dynamicEmail', `postman.demo.${suffix}@northstarbank.example`);
pm.collectionVariables.set('dynamicTaxIdLast4', taxIdLast4);
console.log(`[Northstar Bank] ${pm.info.requestName} -> ${pm.request.method} ${pm.request.url.toString()}`);
pm.test('Contract - response body should be valid JSON', function () {
pm.expect(function () { pm.response.json(); }).to.not.throw();
pm.test('Contract - response should include a correlation ID', function () {
const body = pm.response.json();
const correlationId = body.meta && body.meta.correlationId ? body.meta.correlationId : (body.error && body.error.correlationId ? body.error.correlationId : body.correlationId);
pm.expect(correlationId).to.be.a('string');
pm.expect(correlationId).to.match(/^corr_demo_/);
